name: KPI Strip
purpose: Show the five core BBE signals with value, benchmark, Ahead/Behind, and momentum in a compact read.
guardrails: Do not imply a master brand health score unless an approved source provides one.

name: Momentum Ladder
purpose: Show whether the brand is gaining, holding, declining, or missing momentum evidence across outcome and input metrics.
guardrails: Red momentum must remain visible and plainly labeled.

name: Momentum x Room To Grow Grid
purpose: Cross equity momentum with room-to-grow context to help size urgency and strategic upside.
guardrails: Do not substitute Ahead/Behind for room-to-grow inputs when penetration, share, or category growth data is missing.

name: SMD Driver Map
purpose: Explain how Salient, Meaningful, and Different reinforce or contradict the observed outcome pattern.
guardrails: Do not treat SMD as three siloed scorecards when synthesizing the why.

name: Evidence Ledger
purpose: Show supporting evidence, complicating evidence, source periods, source files, and missing evidence behind a read.
guardrails: Evidence should be visible behind each diagnosis, provocation, and treatment recommendation.

name: Data Basis Inspector
purpose: Show the actual brand packet data, source posture, peer basis, proof labels, and missing inputs behind a request.
guardrails: Show the source metric names and user-facing labels separately when they differ.
Do not fill missing data with assumptions.
Keep reviewed-local, prototype, and approved source states visibly separated.

name: Evidence Spotlight Panel
purpose: Show persisted claim-to-proof continuity across governed turns, including supported claims, missing evidence, guardrail claims, reviewed working context, source candidates, and disabled canonical claim promotion.
guardrails: Evidence spotlight is a claim-to-proof audit, not a new source of truth.
Missing evidence and reviewed working context must remain visible instead of being smoothed into supported claims.
This view cannot promote source candidates, create canonical facts, or enable unsupported claim generation.

name: Diagnosis Trace Summary
purpose: Summarize the deterministic rule that fired, matched conditions, missing conditions, counter-evidence, and treatment links.
guardrails: Expose enough logic for trust without encouraging users to game scoring.

name: Growth Provocation List
purpose: Show prioritized what / so what / now what provocations with evidence, urgency, horizon, and caveats.
guardrails: Provocations are questions or paths to test, not final commands.

name: Treatment Path Card
purpose: Show the first governed treatment recommendation to pressure-test, ranking reasons, proof signals, contraindications, and follow-up signals.
guardrails: Treatment language must remain options to consider or paths to test.

name: Peer Comparison
purpose: Compare brands or competitors using approved peer-set context, pattern similarity, and source caveats.
guardrails: Surface peer-set size and avoid cannibalization or portfolio migration claims without measured evidence.

name: Pattern Radar Brief
purpose: Show similar brands, recurring patterns, evidence gaps, and treatment memory as associative portfolio intelligence.
guardrails: Always state that pattern similarity is associative, not causal.

name: Source Readiness Panel
purpose: Show Momentum and Brand Strategic Context source-owner readiness checks, blockers, required sources, source labels, and executive-use caveats.
guardrails: Do not treat prototype-reviewed or reviewed-local source records as canonical executive-use evidence.
Name the required source-owner extract or approved brand strategy source before closing a readiness blocker.

name: Source Runtime Ingestion Panel
purpose: Show source-owner runtime file-drop coverage, missing file kinds, governance blockers, and disabled default runtime source wiring before any source files can become canonical runtime evidence.
guardrails: Source runtime ingestion inspection cannot wire source-owner files into the default runtime source path.
Audited source-owner files are not canonical facts or answer evidence until source-owner approval and canonical-use governance clear.
Runtime source auto-consumption, file-drop consumption, canonical source writes, and source-data writes remain disabled.

name: Data Gap Panel
purpose: Make unavailable or incomplete views explicit and explain what source would improve the read.
guardrails: Do not fill missing data with unsupported assumptions.

name: Learning Explainer
purpose: Teach a concept with plain language, a brand example, a visual cue, and common misreads.
guardrails: Teaching examples must be clearly separated from measured brand facts.

name: Quiz Card
purpose: Ask the user to classify a read, interpret a signal, or choose the next evidence lens.
guardrails: Explain the answer and the overclaim risk after the user responds.

name: Meeting Takeaway Panel
purpose: Capture a review-required meeting takeaway with provisional decision, evidence, unresolved gaps, and next proof signal.
guardrails: Separate captured decisions from AI suggestions.
Meeting takeaways require human review before final circulation.

name: QBR Story Draft
purpose: Render a draft executive narrative, talk track, slide outline, or agency brief from accepted evidence and provocations.
guardrails: Drafts require human review before circulation.

name: Review Workflow Panel
purpose: Show the governed local review queue, reviewed decisions, blocked capability gates, and disabled approval/write/export states for the active session.
guardrails: Review workflow state is prototype-local and not official enterprise approval.
Blocked capability gates require governance/config changes and cannot be cleared by local review.
Accepted memory remains working context, not source truth.

name: Memory Audit Panel
purpose: Show session memory continuity, accepted working context, review decisions, audit coverage, and disabled memory-write promotion paths without accepting or promoting memory.
guardrails: Memory audit inspection is read-only and cannot accept, edit, reject, or promote memory.
Accepted memory is working context only; it is not source truth, official approval, or canonical brand knowledge.
Auto-accept memory, reviewed-memory writes, canonical memory writes, and enterprise memory storage remain disabled.

name: Audit Trail Panel
purpose: Show persisted runtime audit continuity across turn lifecycle, evidence use, view requests, artifacts, memory suggestions, source governance, runtime quality, latest records, and disabled audit export/canonical-write/enterprise-store paths.
guardrails: Audit trail inspection cannot export audit logs, create official approvals, or write canonical records.
Audit records are prototype local JSON runtime evidence; they do not certify production compliance.
Enterprise audit storage, canonical audit writes, and audit export remain disabled until governance approves them.

name: Review Identity Panel
purpose: Show prototype review identity, local reviewer-label limits, related gates, blocked enterprise approval types, required identity/access steps, and disabled official-approval paths.
guardrails: Review identity inspection is read-only and cannot authenticate users, assign enterprise roles, or create official approvals.
The prototype human_review label supports local workflow only; it is not accountable enterprise identity.
Enterprise identity, role access, brand access control, audit export, and official approval remain disabled until governance approves them.

name: Pilot Learning Panel
purpose: Show reviewed-only pilot learning signals, session-level signal counts, blocked learning paths, and next proof needs without promoting them to outcome learning or canonical memory/source truth.
guardrails: Learning signals are review inputs, not treatment outcome evidence.
Do not convert learning signals into accepted pattern memory, source truth, or canonical writes.
Autonomous learning and outcome learning remain disabled.

name: Quiet Proactivity Panel
purpose: Show review-required follow-up suggestions, held notices, related gates/artifacts/gaps, and disabled reminder/notification/autonomous-action rails.
guardrails: Follow-up suggestions are not scheduled work.
Reminder creation, background checks, external sends, and autonomous actions remain disabled.
Suggestions require human review before becoming accepted memory, artifacts, or source actions.

name: Voice Readiness Panel
purpose: Show governed push-to-talk state, provider adapter readiness, voice orchestration promotion gates, interruption limits, and blocked full-voice capabilities.
guardrails: Push-to-talk readiness is not approval for continuous listening.
Realtime voice and TTS remain gated until runtime parity, consent, privacy, interruption, and storage blockers are cleared.
Voice cannot bypass approved ExperiencePlan views, evidence, memory, audit, or confirmation gates.

name: Provider Adapter Panel
purpose: Show governed provider adapter readiness across text reasoning, SSE streaming, browser speech-to-text prototype input, Realtime candidate gates, TTS disabled state, and provider-bypass blockers.
guardrails: Provider adapter inspection is read-only and cannot connect a provider, enable Realtime voice, enable TTS, or bypass the governed runtime.
Ready text/SSE adapters are prototype runtime paths, not production provider certification.
Browser speech-to-text remains prototype client-side input; continuous voice, autonomous speaking, Realtime runtime connection, and TTS remain gated or disabled.

name: Capability Readiness Panel
purpose: Show disabled risky capability flags, blocked confirmation gates, admin override requirements, and hard-disabled export, circulation, memory-write, source-write, source-promotion, external-ingest, continuous-voice, and runtime-bypass paths.
guardrails: Capability readiness inspection is read-only and cannot enable disabled capabilities or clear blocked gates.
Local review cannot approve blocked capability gates that require governance or config changes.

Export, circulation, memory writes, source writes, source promotion, external ingest, continuous voice, and runtime bypass remain disabled until their capability flags and governance gates are approved.

name: Persistence Readiness Panel
purpose: Show browser/local JSON persistence state, review-action readiness, accepted memory context, source-candidate non-canonical posture, and blocked enterprise promotion requirements.
guardrails: Local persistence readiness is not enterprise persistence approval.
Canonical writes and source runtime auto-consumption remain disabled.
Enterprise schema, identity/access, retention/privacy, backup/recovery, and canonical promotion blockers must remain visible.

name: Treatment Outcome Readiness Panel
purpose: Show treatment outcome learning promotion gates, related treatment paths and follow-up signals, efficacy-rule blockers, portfolio learning storage blockers, and disabled outcome-learning states.
guardrails: Outcome readiness is not proof that a treatment happened or worked.
Follow-up signals are not causal treatment evidence without approved methodology.
Outcome learning, treatment efficacy claims, accepted outcome records, and canonical learning writes remain disabled.

name: Source Promotion Readiness Panel
purpose: Show reviewed-local source promotion records, extracted source claims, source-review gates, canonical fact blockers, and disabled runtime source consumption.
guardrails: Reviewed-local source candidates are not canonical facts.
Extracted source claims are not runtime evidence.
Canonical writes and runtime auto-consumption remain disabled until source-owner and enterprise governance approve promotion.

name: Artifact Readiness Panel
purpose: Show generated artifact readiness, reviewer roles, evidence/source-view coverage, language approvals, circulation blockers, and disabled export gates.
guardrails: Artifact readiness inspection cannot enable export, copy, circulation, or official approval.
Generated artifacts require human review before circulation.
Export remains blocked until stakeholder language, artifact shape, and capability gates are approved.

name: Experience Architecture Panel
purpose: Show ExperiencePlan template coverage, audience/objective support, approved skill/view inventory, dynamic UI guardrails, and workspace composition blockers.
guardrails: Experience architecture inspection cannot generate arbitrary UI or unregistered views.
Role-specific workspaces must be composed from approved templates, skills, views, and evidence needs.
Unsupported audiences, objectives, metrics, or artifacts must fail closed into review or data-gap states.

name: Canvas Continuity Panel
purpose: Show persisted canvas continuity across approved rendered views, fallbacks, focused views, compatible voice-canvas views, proof rails, visible presence signals, interruption recovery, and disabled arbitrary UI/voice capabilities.
guardrails: Canvas continuity inspection cannot generate arbitrary UI, unregistered views, unsupported metrics, or new source claims.
The canvas can preserve approved rendered state across interruptions, but it cannot expose private reasoning or enable server-side provider cancellation.
Continuous listening, background wake word, autonomous speaking, continuous voice barge-in, and arbitrary view IDs remain disabled.

name: Runtime Governance Panel
purpose: Show governed runtime surface readiness, default versus opt-in state, kill-switch posture, disabled capabilities, provider gates, and promotion blockers.
guardrails: Runtime governance inspection cannot enable disabled capabilities or alter runtime policy.
Ready surfaces are governed prototype surfaces, not production approvals.
Gated Realtime, continuous voice, TTS, export, source write, and canonical promotion states must stay visible.

name: Runtime Quality Panel
purpose: Show persisted runtime self-check coverage, pass/watch/blocked quality counts, latest checks, human-review-required items, and consistency guardrails before any surface or capability is promoted.
guardrails: Runtime quality inspection cannot override, dismiss, or mark a quality check as passed.
Watch and blocked checks are promotion blockers until the underlying evidence, governance, or runtime gap is resolved.
Passing checks do not permit exports, canonical writes, full voice, provider bypass, arbitrary UI, or official approval.

name: Foundation Readiness Panel
purpose: Show cross-rail Brand Growth Intelligence foundation readiness across approved experience architecture, evidence grounding, review/memory, source governance, audit/quality, runtime, surfaces, providers, voice, persistence, artifacts, and outcome-readiness.
guardrails: Foundation readiness inspection cannot enable enterprise persistence, official approvals, canonical writes, exports, full voice, autonomous learning, or arbitrary UI generation.
Ready and prototype states are promotion evidence, not production authorization.
Gated paths must remain visible beside the foundation readiness signal.

name: Executive Pilot Runbook Panel
purpose: Show the read-only CMO/sponsor pilot sequence, proof moments, governed workspace assembly, funding asks, and disabled promotion gates from the current Brand Growth Intelligence foundation.
guardrails: The pilot runbook is inspection-only and cannot be exported, circulated, or treated as an official funding document.
Runbook steps must use approved ExperiencePlan templates, skills, and dynamic views.
Source, persistence, voice, artifact, approval, learning, and arbitrary-UI gates must remain visible.

name: Promotion Gate Panel
purpose: Show whether the current foundation is ready for CMO demo, ready for pilot review, or still production-blocked, with demo proof, critical gates, enabled demo rails, next pilot steps, and funding rationale.
guardrails: Promotion gate inspection cannot certify production readiness or approve funding by itself.
CMO-demo and pilot-review readiness are prototype promotion signals, not permission to enable gated capabilities.
Enterprise persistence, official approvals, canonical writes, artifact export, full voice, autonomous learning, and arbitrary UI generation must remain blocked until governance clears them.
